Title: Getting Started with Campaign Intelligence
Summary: A step-by-step guide to setting up your first campaign in Campaign Intelligence, including channel configuration, budget allocation, and launch checklist.
ArticleBody:
## Getting Started with Campaign Intelligence

Campaign Intelligence is Catalyst's multi-channel campaign planning and execution module. This article walks you through setting up your first campaign.

### Prerequisites
- Admin access to your Catalyst organisation
- At least one connected ad channel (Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, or Programmatic)
- A campaign budget approved in your account plan

### Step 1 — Create a Campaign
1. Navigate to **Campaigns > New Campaign**
2. Enter a Campaign Name, Start Date, and End Date
3. Select the primary channel from the Channel dropdown
4. Set your Total Budget and allocate by channel if running multi-channel

### Step 2 — Connect Your Channels
Each channel requires a connector credential. Navigate to **Settings > Integrations** and follow the OAuth flow for each platform. Connector status is shown as a green tick when active.

### Step 3 — Configure Targeting
Use the Audience selector to attach a saved Audience Studio segment, or define a new audience inline using demographics, firmographics, or intent signals.

### Step 4 — Launch
Click **Review & Launch**. Campaign Intelligence runs a pre-launch validation check. Any warnings (budget pacing, missing creative) are surfaced before you confirm.

### Next Steps
- Set up automated reporting in Attribution Engine
- Schedule a weekly performance digest in your notification preferences

If you need help at any step, open a support case or use the Aria chat assistant.

Title: Setting Up Attribution Engine Multi-Touch Models
Summary: How to configure multi-touch attribution models in Attribution Engine, choose between linear, time-decay, and data-driven models, and export ROI reports.
ArticleBody:
## Setting Up Attribution Engine Multi-Touch Models

Attribution Engine supports four attribution models. This article explains how to configure them and interpret the results.

### Available Models

| Model | Description | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| **Last Touch** | 100% credit to the final touchpoint | Brand campaigns with short sales cycles |
| **Linear** | Equal credit across all touchpoints | Awareness plays with long consideration windows |
| **Time Decay** | More credit to recent touchpoints | High-velocity B2B deals |
| **Data-Driven** | ML model trained on your conversion data | Accounts with 1,000+ conversions/month |

### Configuring Your Model
1. Go to **Attribution > Model Configuration**
2. Select your Conversion Event (form fill, demo request, closed-won opportunity)
3. Choose your attribution window (default: 90 days)
4. Select the model type
5. Click **Apply Model**

Attribution Engine processes historical data retroactively for the chosen window. Results appear in the Attribution dashboard within 24 hours.

### Exporting Reports
Navigate to **Reports > Attribution Summary** and click **Export**. Supported formats: CSV, PDF, Salesforce Report sync.

### Data-Driven Model Requirements
The data-driven model requires a minimum of 1,000 attributed conversions in the past 90 days. If your account does not meet this threshold, the model selector will show a yellow warning — use Time Decay as an alternative.

Contact support if you see discrepancies greater than 15% between Attribution Engine and your ad platform native reporting.

Title: How to Build an Audience Segment in Audience Studio
Summary: Create, refine, and export audience segments using Audience Studio's visual segment builder, including first-party data uploads and intent signal enrichment.
ArticleBody:
## How to Build an Audience Segment in Audience Studio

Audience Studio lets you combine first-party CRM data, third-party intent signals, and behavioural data into reusable audience segments.

### Opening the Segment Builder
Navigate to **Audience Studio > Segments > New Segment**.

### Adding Conditions
Conditions are grouped into AND/OR logic blocks:
- **AND** — the audience member must meet all conditions in the group
- **OR** — the audience member meets at least one condition

**Example:** Target accounts with more than 200 employees AND in the Technology industry who have visited your pricing page in the last 30 days.

### Data Sources Available
- **CRM Sync** — Salesforce Account, Contact, Lead fields (syncs every 4 hours)
- **Intent Signals** — G2, Bombora, and TechTarget intent topics
- **Web Behaviour** — Page visits, form fills, content downloads (requires pixel installed)
- **Uploaded Lists** — CSV upload of matched email or domain lists

### Saving and Using Segments
1. Click **Save Segment** and give it a descriptive name
2. Segments are available immediately in Campaign Intelligence for targeting
3. Segment size estimate appears in the right panel (refreshes every 6 hours)

### Exporting a Segment
Go to **Actions > Export** to download a matched list as CSV, or push directly to a connected ad platform via **Activate > Platform Push**.

Note: Segment sizes over 500,000 profiles may take up to 2 hours to process.

Title: Connecting Salesforce CRM to Catalyst
Summary: Step-by-step instructions for installing the Catalyst managed package, configuring the Salesforce connector, and verifying bi-directional data sync.
ArticleBody:
## Connecting Salesforce CRM to Catalyst

Catalyst integrates natively with Salesforce Sales Cloud via a managed package. This article covers the installation and configuration steps.

### Before You Start
- You must be a Salesforce System Administrator
- Your Catalyst subscription must include the CRM Connector (available on Professional and Enterprise tiers)
- Ensure your Salesforce org is on API version 55.0 or later

### Step 1 — Install the Managed Package
1. Log in to Catalyst as an Admin
2. Go to **Settings > Integrations > Salesforce**
3. Click **Install Managed Package** — this opens the Salesforce AppExchange installer
4. Select **Install for All Users** and accept the package permissions
5. Return to Catalyst and click **Verify Installation**

### Step 2 — Authorise the Connection
1. Click **Connect Salesforce Org**
2. Complete the OAuth flow — log in with your Salesforce System Administrator credentials
3. Approve the requested permissions (Read/Write on Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities, Campaigns)

### Step 3 — Configure Field Mappings
Navigate to **Settings > Integrations > Salesforce > Field Mappings**. The default mappings cover standard fields. Add custom field mappings for any vertical-specific fields.

### Step 4 — Verify Sync
1. Navigate to a test Contact in Salesforce — you should see a **Catalyst** section on the record page showing campaign engagement history
2. In Catalyst, navigate to **Audience Studio > CRM Sync** — the sync status should show **Active** with the last sync timestamp

### Sync Frequency
- Contacts, Leads: every 4 hours
- Accounts, Opportunities: every 6 hours
- Opportunity stage updates: near real-time (webhook)

If the sync shows errors, check the **Integration Logs** in Catalyst Settings for detailed error messages.

Title: Troubleshooting Attribution Discrepancies
Summary: Common reasons for attribution data discrepancies between Catalyst Attribution Engine and ad platform native reporting, and how to resolve them.
ArticleBody:
## Troubleshooting Attribution Discrepancies

It is normal to see some difference between Catalyst Attribution Engine and ad platform (Google, Meta, LinkedIn) native reporting. This article explains common causes and resolutions.

### Expected Variance
A variance of up to 10–15% is expected due to:
- Different attribution windows (platform default vs. your configured window)
- Cross-device matching (Catalyst uses deterministic matching; platforms use probabilistic)
- View-through vs. click-through credit differences

### Common Issues

#### Issue 1 — Large discrepancy (>20%) in Google Ads conversions
**Cause:** Google's auto-tagging may be disabled, preventing GCLID capture.
**Fix:** In Google Ads, go to **Account Settings > Auto-Tagging** and enable it. Allow 48 hours for data to reprocess.

#### Issue 2 — Meta conversions showing zero in Attribution Engine
**Cause:** The Meta Pixel is not firing on the thank-you / confirmation page.
**Fix:** Verify pixel installation on the conversion page using the Meta Pixel Helper browser extension. Confirm the conversion event name matches what is configured in Catalyst (**Settings > Conversion Events**).

#### Issue 3 — LinkedIn conversions under-reported
**Cause:** LinkedIn uses a 30-day view-through window by default; Catalyst defaults to click-through only.
**Fix:** In Catalyst, go to **Attribution > Model Configuration > Advanced** and enable **Include View-Through** for LinkedIn.

#### Issue 4 — Opportunities not appearing in ROI reports
**Cause:** The Salesforce CRM sync may be delayed or the Opportunity stage is not in the configured closed-won mapping.
**Fix:** Check **Settings > Integrations > Salesforce > Sync Logs** for errors. Verify that the closed-won stage name in Salesforce exactly matches the value in **Attribution > Conversion Events > Salesforce Opportunity Stage**.

### Still Seeing Issues?
Open a support case with your Attribution Engine report export and the platform native report for the same date range. Our support team will review within your SLA window.

Title: Content Hub — Uploading and Organising Assets
Summary: How to upload creative assets to Content Hub, organise them into collections, apply metadata tags, and share assets with campaign managers.
ArticleBody:
## Content Hub — Uploading and Organising Assets

Content Hub is Catalyst's digital asset management module. This article explains how to upload, organise, and share creative assets.

### Supported File Types
- Images: JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP, SVG (max 50 MB per file)
- Video: MP4, MOV, AVI (max 2 GB per file)
- Documents: PDF, DOCX, PPTX (max 100 MB per file)
- HTML5 display: ZIP bundle (max 10 MB)

### Uploading Assets
1. Navigate to **Content Hub > Upload**
2. Drag and drop files or click **Browse**
3. Add metadata: Asset Name, Campaign Tag, Channel, Language, Expiry Date
4. Click **Upload**

Bulk uploads of up to 500 files are supported via the bulk upload panel.

### Organising into Collections
Collections are folders for grouping related assets:
1. Go to **Content Hub > Collections > New Collection**
2. Name the collection (e.g. Q3 Campaign — Display UK)
3. Drag assets into the collection, or use **Add to Collection** from the asset detail view

### Tagging and Searchability
Apply tags from the asset detail view. Tags are searchable across Content Hub and surfaced in Campaign Intelligence when selecting creative.

### Sharing Assets
Use **Share > Generate Link** to produce a read-only shareable URL (expires in 7 or 30 days). For internal team members, use **Share > Assign to User** to grant access without a link.

### Asset Versions
When uploading a revised version of an existing asset, open the original asset and click **Upload New Version**. Version history is retained and previous versions remain accessible.

Title: Understanding Your Health Score
Summary: What the Catalyst Health Score measures, how it is calculated, what score ranges mean for your account, and actions you can take to improve it.
ArticleBody:
## Understanding Your Health Score

Your Catalyst Health Score is a 0–100 index that reflects your account's overall adoption, engagement, and outcomes. It is visible on your portal dashboard and updated weekly.

### What Goes Into the Score

| Component | Weight | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Platform Usage Index | 30% | Logins, feature activations, campaigns run in the past 30 days |
| Campaign Performance | 25% | Click-through rate, conversion rate vs. benchmark |
| Attribution Coverage | 20% | Percentage of spend with attribution tracking enabled |
| Support Case Volume | 15% | Frequency and severity of open cases |
| CSAT | 10% | Average satisfaction score from post-case surveys |

### Score Ranges

| Range | Status | What it means |
|---|---|---|
| 80–100 | Excellent | Strong adoption and performance — on track for renewal |
| 60–79 | Good | Healthy overall with some improvement areas |
| 40–59 | At Risk | Low usage or performance issues — Customer Success will reach out |
| 0–39 | Critical | Significant intervention needed |

### How to Improve Your Score
- **Increase usage:** Log in and run campaigns consistently. Idle accounts score lower.
- **Enable attribution:** Connect all active channels to Attribution Engine.
- **Reduce open cases:** Resolve outstanding support cases. High open case counts reduce the support component score.
- **Respond to surveys:** CSAT scores are only counted when surveys are completed.

### Viewing Your Score Trend
Navigate to **Portal > Dashboard > Health Score** to see a 12-week trend chart.

Your Customer Success Manager reviews accounts with scores below 60 every fortnight. If your score has dropped unexpectedly, contact your CSM or open a support case.

Title: Onboarding Checklist — Enterprise Tier
Summary: The complete onboarding checklist for Enterprise tier customers, covering technical setup, user provisioning, training, and go-live verification steps.
ArticleBody:
## Onboarding Checklist — Enterprise Tier

This article is your reference guide for the Catalyst Enterprise onboarding journey. Your onboarding is tracked in the Catalyst portal under **My Onboarding**.

### Phase 1 — Technical Setup (Week 1–2)
- [ ] Salesforce managed package installed and CRM sync verified
- [ ] All active ad platforms connected (Google, Meta, LinkedIn, programmatic DSP)
- [ ] Catalyst tracking pixel deployed on all key web properties
- [ ] Conversion events configured and test conversions verified
- [ ] Admin users provisioned (max 5 Admin seats on Enterprise)
- [ ] SSO configured (if applicable — Okta, Azure AD, or SAML 2.0)

### Phase 2 — Data Configuration (Week 2–3)
- [ ] First-party audience segment built in Audience Studio
- [ ] Attribution model selected and historical data processed
- [ ] Field mappings verified for CRM sync (Leads, Contacts, Opportunities)
- [ ] Content Hub collections set up and assets uploaded

### Phase 3 — Training (Week 3–4)
- [ ] Campaign Intelligence training session completed (Campaign Manager role)
- [ ] Attribution Engine training completed (Analytics / RevOps role)
- [ ] Audience Studio training completed (Demand Gen role)
- [ ] Admin training completed (IT / RevOps role)

Training sessions are booked through your onboarding project in the portal.

### Phase 4 — Go-Live (Week 4)
- [ ] First live campaign launched in Campaign Intelligence
- [ ] Attribution dashboard showing data for at least one channel
- [ ] Health Score above 60
- [ ] Kickoff call with Customer Success Manager completed

### Tracking Progress
Your onboarding completion percentage is visible on the portal dashboard and updated in real time as steps are marked complete.

Contact your onboarding specialist if any phase is blocked — the expected onboarding duration for Enterprise is 4–6 weeks.

Title: Managing User Access and Permissions
Summary: How to invite users, assign roles, configure permission levels, and remove access in your Catalyst account.
ArticleBody:
## Managing User Access and Permissions

This article covers user management for Catalyst account administrators.

### User Roles

| Role | Access Level |
|---|---|
| **Admin** | Full access to all modules, settings, and billing |
| **Campaign Manager** | Create and manage campaigns; read-only access to Attribution and Audience Studio |
| **Analyst** | Read-only access to Attribution Engine and reporting; no campaign management |
| **Viewer** | Dashboard and report view only; no create/edit permissions |

### Inviting a New User
1. Go to **Settings > Users > Invite User**
2. Enter the user's email address
3. Select their role
4. Click **Send Invitation**

The user receives an email invitation valid for 72 hours. If they do not accept, resend from the same screen.

### Seat Limits by Subscription Tier

| Tier | Admin | Campaign Manager | Analyst | Viewer |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Starter | 1 | 2 | 1 | Unlimited |
| Professional | 2 | 5 | 3 | Unlimited |
| Enterprise | 5 | Unlimited | Unlimited | Unlimited |

### Editing a User's Role
Go to **Settings > Users**, click the user's name, and change their role from the dropdown. Changes take effect immediately.

### Removing Access
Click **Deactivate** on the user record. Deactivated users lose access immediately. Their historical activity is retained for audit purposes. Seats are released within 24 hours.

### SSO and SCIM Provisioning
Enterprise accounts can configure SCIM provisioning for automatic user lifecycle management. Navigate to **Settings > Security > SCIM** for setup instructions.

Title: How to Submit and Track a Support Case
Summary: Instructions for submitting a support case through the Catalyst portal, setting priority and category, tracking progress, and understanding SLA response times.
ArticleBody:
## How to Submit and Track a Support Case

The fastest way to get help is through the Catalyst Client Portal. This article explains how to create and monitor your support cases.

### Submitting a New Case
1. Log in to the Catalyst Client Portal
2. Click **Support > New Case** in the navigation
3. Complete the case form:
   - **Subject:** Brief one-line description of the issue
   - **Category:** Select the most relevant category (Technical, Billing, Feature Request, Onboarding, General)
   - **Affected Module:** Select the Catalyst module affected (Campaign Intelligence, Attribution Engine, Audience Studio, Content Hub)
   - **Urgency:** Select Low, Medium, or High based on business impact
   - **Description:** Provide as much detail as possible — steps to reproduce, screenshots, error messages
4. Click **Submit Case**

### Response Times by Subscription Tier

| Tier | First Response | Resolution Target |
|---|---|---|
| Enterprise | 1 business hour | 4 business hours |
| Professional | 4 business hours | 2 business days |
| Starter | 8 business hours | 5 business days |

Response times apply to business hours (Monday–Friday, 9am–6pm in your account's time zone).

### Tracking Your Cases
Go to **Support > My Cases** to see all open and closed cases. Each case shows its current status:
- **New** — received, not yet assigned
- **In Progress** — actively being worked on by our support team
- **Waiting on Customer** — we need more information from you
- **Resolved** — solution provided; case closes after 5 business days if no response

### Providing Additional Information
Open the case and click **Add Comment** to provide updates, screenshots, or log files. Avoid opening duplicate cases — adding a comment re-opens a resolved case automatically.

### Escalating a Case
If a case has not been responded to within your SLA window, click **Escalate** on the case record. This flags the case to a senior support engineer and notifies your Customer Success Manager.
